mission.title: argo: Support web shell and enable user to exec into pod from argocd UI
mission.description: Support web shell and enable user to exec into pod from argocd UI. This issue affects 77+ users.

steps.title: Identify argo feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your argo deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo -l app=argo
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo -n argo --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Support web shell and enable user to exec into pod from argocd UI

steps.title: Check current Pod configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo resources:
```bash
kubectl get pod -A
kubectl describe pod <name> -n argo
```
What change you think needs making.

ArgoCD needs to support web shell or EXEC into pod.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support web shell and enable user to exec into pod from
steps.description:
fixes https://github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/issues/4351

Note on DCO:

If the DCO action in the integration test fails, one or more of your commits are not signed off. Please click on the *Details* link next to the DCO action for instructions on how to resolve this.

Checklist:

* [ ] Either (a) I've created an [enhancement proposal](https://github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/issues/new/choose) and discussed it with the community, (b) this is a bug fix, or (c) this does not need to be in the release

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm Support web shell and enable user to exec into is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo -n argo --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n argo --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.
